title: euphorbia dulcis var. deseglisei
intro: Euphorbia dulcis var. deseglisei, also known as the cane stick or candelabra tree, is a variety of the Euphorbia dulcis plant, which is a succulent species within the Euphorbiaceae family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its sap, which contains latex that has been used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds. The medicinal properties of Euphorbia dulcis var. deseglisei have been attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it beneficial for the treatment of conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor cuts and scrapes. In herbal preparations, the sap is often extracted and used to create topical ointments, salves, and creams that can be applied directly to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
